sampling_note: Two-plus replays per variant covering wins and timeouts: baseline=[1 win T9, 4 timeout, 2 timeout for queen-HP-trace], rush=[1 win T6, 8 timeout T5], turtle=[1 win T22, 8 timeout T21], flank=[1 win T8, 2 timeout, 8 timeout/draw, 14 timeout]. Plus aggregate damage/round-count surveys across first 30 seeds per variant. The four variants now have distinguishable win-rate signatures (baseline 75%, rush 77%, turtle 77%, flank 62%) and the iter-1 'pathfinders 1-round wipe' failure mode no longer dominates timeouts.

per_replay_scores.notes: Win at T9 (spider-web tick 141). Canonical floor capture chain. T8 pathfinders attack web-guard (3 rounds, 6 ants dead, 2 elites dead) — queen takes only 6 dmg net with 3 web-mend heals (HP 41 -> 38 over 4 rounds). T8 vanguard-bravo follow-up wins 3-round battle (HP 38 -> 18; in round 1 queen drops below 50% threshold, mend stops). T9 finisher 3 rounds (HP 18 -> 0). Web-mend visible in trace: rounds 0-1 of pathfinders fight queen at frac=0.95 -> heal=1 each, then mend stops naturally as queen drops below 0.5 mid-second-fight. Composition diversity intact (potato-bug u0020 lands {2,4,3,3} vs queen).

Timeout. T8 pathfinders fail web-guard 3 rounds. T8 vanguard-bravo fails web-guard 5 rounds (5 ants dead, 1 elite dead) — queen ends fight at HP 30/41. NO 1-round pathfinders wipe (cf. iter-1 baseline-4 which had rounds=1, 6 dead vs 0). Subsequent dead-air tail still ~88 turns. Outcome 1 (battles felt contested but the no-redeployment dead-air still reads as engine-ate-your-orders). Iter-1's 1-round-cliff failure mode is broken; what remains is ant-side AI not redeploying.

Timeout. FOUR queen battles: T8 pathfinders lose 3r (queen HP 41->38), T8 vanguard-bravo wins 3r (38->18), T9 vanguard-bravo wins 3r (18->2), T10 vanguard-bravo loses 3r (queen finishes the 2-survivor party). Queen ends scenario at HP=2 — meaningful near-miss. Web-mend total heals applied: 5. Tactical-variety bumped to 3 because the four-battle escalation reads as a real war of attrition: ants kill ALL FOUR elites and chip queen to 2 HP but can't close the deal. This is exactly the texture variance the iter-1 prompt asked for. Outcome would be credible if the engine ended the scenario at the wipe instead of running 90 dead turns.

Win at T6. Climb-bypass opening unchanged from iter-1. Web-guard battles now slightly different shape: T5 pathfinders 3r (queen HP 41->38, 3 mend heals, 1 elite kill); T5 vanguard-bravo wins 3r (38->18); T6 finishes 1r. Queen kill-battle is single round with potato-bug landing 4 dmg twice — feels decisive.

Timeout, antPostsAtEnd=1 (climb-bypass strategy holds storm-drain only). T5 pathfinders fail 3r, queen HP 41->38 (3 heals). T5 vanguard-bravo fails 5r — 5 ants dead, all 4 elites dead, queen ends at HP=18 with 5 heals applied across the fight. Compare iter-1 rush-8 where T6 pathfinders attempt ended in a rounds=1, 6-dead, 0-spider wipe — that 1-round cliff is gone. Outcome credibility 2 (loss is an attritional defeat, not a dice-cliff). Dead-air tail unchanged.

Win at T22. Long-siege opening as iter-1; ant-side kill is now 5-round attrition fight (rare in iter-1 sample). Mend visible early then naturally cuts off mid-fight. Patience-payoff narrative still works.

Timeout. Same web-guard battles as rush-8 but time-shifted (T21 instead of T5). Queen ends at HP=18 after 5-round vanguard-bravo fight. Iter-1 turtle-8 had pathfinders 1-round wipe — gone. Composition still does its job (potato-bug landing 2-4 vs queen). Outcome credibility raised because losses are attritional not catastrophic.

Win at T9. Three distinct routes intact (pathfinders west-edge, vanguard-bravo east-edge, vanguard-alpha canonical floor). FIVE queen battles in this replay: T6 pathfinders fail 4r, T7 vanguard-bravo wins 3r, T8 vanguard-bravo wins 3r (queen drops to 0 HP but battle ends without queen kill flag — interesting edge), T9 vanguard-bravo finishes queen 1r with potato-bug landing dmg=6 (6 because elites are gone, defenseBonus drops). Tactical-variety bumped to 3 because the pincer/attrition rhythm is uniquely flank-shaped — pathfinders absorb the first wave, vanguard-bravo arrives from the opposite corner one turn later. Two web-watch encounters create extra tension en route.

Timeout. T6 pathfinders lose web-guard 3r (queen 41->38, mend 1 each round 1-2). T7 pathfinders survivors hit WEB-WATCH (route exposure) — they win 4r. T7 vanguard-bravo wins web-guard 3r (queen 38->22, 3 heals). T8 vanguard-bravo loses web-guard 3r — queen ends at HP=7. Five distinct battles. Queen survives by 7 HP — without web-mend (4 heals over 6 round-starts), queen would be at HP=3. The mend is buying the queen meaningful slack.

Timeout, with a 'draw' outcome on T8. T7 vanguard-bravo wins web-guard 5r (queen 41->13, 5 heals). T8 vanguard-bravo vs web-guard ends in DRAW — 5 rounds, queen drops to HP=1 in round 2 then HP=0 round 3 but combat continues with attackers also dying; result is recorded as 'draw'. This is the cliffhanger texture the iter-1 prompt described. Watching this replay you see 'queen has 1 HP and the last footman has 1 HP, who hits first' — the dice-cliff is now narratively interesting rather than binary.

Timeout. T6 pathfinders lose web-guard 3r. T7 pathfinders fail web-watch (this is the flank-specific exposure: web-watch hits unguarded survivors). T7 vanguard-bravo wins web-guard 4r (queen 38->19, 4 heals). T8 vanguard-bravo fails web-watch — surviving vanguard-bravo is too thin. Queen ends at 19 HP. Outcome 1 because the flank-specific failure mode (web-watch ambushing rear-element en route) is plausibly tactical but the player has no in-replay agency to react.

findings.observation: Three of four variants are now in the [65,80] band: baseline 75% (delta -9pp vs iter-1's 84%), rush 77% (-13pp), turtle 77% (-13pp). Flank is at 62% (3pp below floor). The iter-1 HIGH (all four above 80%) is RESOLVED. The web-mend mechanic produced the asked-for texture variance: in 30-seed surveys per variant, queen-kill battles are now spread across 1-5 rounds (baseline distribution {1:8, 2:8, 3:8, 4:4, 5:2}; iter-1's effectively-only-1-round cliff is gone). Timeouts no longer feature the 'pathfinders rounds=1, 6 ants dead, 0 spider deaths' wipe — across 8 timeouts I traced, all 4 elites die in the typical timeout, and the queen ends scenarios at HP {0,2,7,9,13,18,18,19,...}. flank-2 ends at HP=7, flank-8 records a 'draw' battle, baseline-2 ends at HP=2. Web-mend heals fire 3-7 times per multi-battle sequence and naturally turn off when the queen dips below 50% (29 ticks in 30-seed sample where the heal stopped mid-battle and let attackers close). Why flank dipped: flank's geographic spread routes pathfinder survivors INTO web-watch on T7 (avg 0.43 web-watch battles/replay vs 0.33 baseline), and web-watch is a strict ant-killer. This reads as fun-tactically interesting (flank pays a real route-exposure tax for its earlier capture timing) NOT as a balance bug — flank still wins 62/100, the failure mode is geometrically explicable, and the 3pp dip is small relative to iter-1's 11pp range. Severity downgraded to MEDIUM from iter-1 HIGH because the cliff is broken and the band miss is small and explicable.
findings.suggested_action: Lever 6 (troop mechanics) micro-adjust: bump ant-archer base attack 4 -> 5 (single integer step) so flank's pathfinders, which are archer-heavy, take fewer casualties to web-watch on the way to web-guard. Expected effect: flank ~67%, no change to baseline/rush/turtle (they don't engage web-watch as often). Alternatively, leave at 62% and accept it as the 'risky aggressive' strategy signature — three-of-four-in-band with a 3pp miss may be the right answer for diversity.

findings.observation: Unchanged from iter-1 and consistent with the iter-2 prompt's claim. Across 120 replays sampled (30 per variant), damage to spider-queen by attacker unit type: potato-bug avg 2.99 dmg/strike (distribution {2:244, 3:275, 4:234}), footman avg 1.32 dmg/strike (distribution {1:981, 2:469}), archer 1.00 (n=806, all 1s), mage 1.00 (n=647, all 1s), scout 1.00 (n=322, all 1s). The strict ordering heavy-melee > light-melee > ranged is preserved. Pathfinders (archer/mage/scout heavy) still effectively cannot dent the queen and serve only as a volley-then-die elite-deletion battery. The remaining ~50% of unit roles are floor-1-only against queen, which means archer-heavy parties cannot win the kill-fight on their own. This was the iter-1 medium finding and the prompt didn't address it; iter-2 changes don't change this lever.
findings.suggested_action: Lever 6: give ant-archer's volley a +2 damage bonus vs targets with the 'leader' tag, OR raise ant-archer base attack 4 -> 5 (this also helps flank's win-rate dip). The latter is a single-step change with a measurable downstream effect on both composition diversity AND the flank rate.

findings.observation: Improved vs iter-1. Wins remain credible (rush-1 = speed; turtle-1 = patient siege; baseline-1/flank-1 = coordinated assault). LOSSES are now mostly credible attritional defeats: in 8/8 sampled timeouts, all 4 elites die before the player runs out of attackers, and the queen ends with single-digit-to-teens HP. Iter-1's 'rounds=1, 6 ants dead vs 0 spider deaths' dice-cliff failure is no longer present in the sampled timeouts. flank-8 even produces a 'draw' battle (queen 1 HP, attackers also wiped). What remains: the dead-air tail. After the field force is spent, the engine still runs 70-90 dead turns of turn-start + queen-ultimate-charged events, with vanguard-alpha (or rush's surviving home-base party) alive but never redeployed. To a reader the engine looks idle, which undercuts the otherwise-credible loss. Iter-1 made the same suggestion for an early-loss detector / 4-of-5 holder win condition.
findings.suggested_action: Lever 4 (scenario goal): add 'all ant field parties wiped or unable to reach spider-web within remaining turns' as an early-loss detector that ends the scenario at the wipe turn. Combined with 'hold >=4 of 5 POSTs for 8 consecutive turns' supplementary win condition. Effect: most current timeouts (4-of-5 POSTs held from T5 onward) become wins-by-control; the rest end cleanly at the wipe rather than dragging 80 turns. Win rates would shift up ~5-8pp; band-tightening can reabsorb that with the queen HP knob if needed.

findings.observation: Improved vs iter-1 (decisions_articulable averages 2.36 across the 11 sampled replays vs iter-1's 2.13). Web-mend creates round-by-round HP texture: in baseline-1, queen HP traces 41->39->38->38->38 / 38->34->26->18 / 18->9->2->0; the inflection at HP=20 (where mend stops) is visible to anyone watching. flank-8 produces a queen at 1 HP draw battle. flank-2 has 5 distinct battles per timeout. BUT: ability fires are still scripted prologues — every web-guard fight begins with volley/mend before round 0 attacks (baseline-1 ticks 102/103/105/106; flank-1 ticks 76/77/79/80). 42-46 ability events per replay batch, all volley/mend. Royal Jelly is never fired in any sampled replay. Queen ultimate charges to 100 by T19 and never fires (never reaches the trigger condition). Web-mend's variance lives entirely on the spider side; ant abilities still feel scripted.
findings.suggested_action: Lever 1 (spider AI) + Lever 6 (troop mechanics): (a) add a queen 'web-snare' that triggers when she drops below 50% HP, immobilizing one ant unit for the remainder of the fight — this ALSO replaces web-mend at the threshold so the queen has BOTH a defensive (mend above 50%) and offensive (snare below 50%) phase, doubling the texture variance. (b) auto-fire ant queen-ultimate when charge >=80 AND any ant party is engaged with web-guard — gives the unused 100-charge a destination. Either change is an integer-step troop-mechanics tweak.

findings.observation: Improved vs iter-1 in the BATTLE phase (round-by-round HP variation, mend cliff, near-miss timeouts) but the dead-air tail still drags reading-10-replays-in-a-row. Flank replays in particular benefit: the 5-distinct-battle structure (web-guard, web-watch, web-guard, web-watch, web-guard) reads as a real geographic campaign rather than a single chokepoint dice-roll. baseline-2's 4-battle attrition where queen ends at HP=2 reads as a genuinely close loss. Rush remains the watchability standout (6-turn finishes). Turtle's middle T10-T20 dead air remains. Reading 10 replays in a row: replays 1-6 show new texture (web-mend visible HP swings, draws, near-misses); replays 7-10 still feel repetitive because every web-guard fight opens with the same ant volley/mend prologue. Score holds at 2 — clear improvement from iter-0's 1, not yet 3 because of the prologue and the dead-air tail.
findings.suggested_action: Same as outcome-credibility: implement the early-loss detector (zero AI cost, immediate watchability lift on the 25-38% of replays that timeout). This is the highest-leverage single change for watchability.
